title: Day 63: Senate Confirms April 28 Hearing With Maru Campos; PAN/PRI Defend Chihuahua Governor — CIA Sovereignty Crisis Enters National Political War
detail: Day 63 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). Fractura Total Day 16 (declared April 11): The El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis internal war remains in a sustained dual-buildup phase — no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the Jalisco heartland through Day 16. 46 days to FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match (June 11, Mexico City). CIA SOVEREIGNTY CRISIS — POLITICAL ESCALATION (April 26): The sovereignty crisis ignited by the April 20 CIA vehicle crash in Chihuahua's Sierra Tarahumara advanced to a formal constitutional confrontation on April 26. (1) SENATE HEARING CONFIRMED: The Mexican Senate formally approved a hearing with Chihuahua Governor María Eugenia Campos Galván ('Maru Campos') and Mexico City's Attorney General Ernestina Godoy, scheduled for April 28, 2026 at 11:00 hours before the Constitutional Points and Public Security commissions of the Senate. The hearing is specifically to address CIA agents' participation in the April 20 counter-narcotics operation against Sinaloa Cartel drug labs without federal authorization. Infobae reported Senate leadership declared it 'indispensable to determine whether the federal government authorized the CIA agents' participation in Chihuahua.' (2) PAN POLITICAL DEFENSE: The National Action Party (PAN) issued a formal statement reaffirming its 'absolute and total support' for Governor Maru Campos, signed by PAN president Jorge Romero. PRI also publicly defended the Chihuahua governor. Both parties contested Sheinbaum's characterization of the state government as 'the main failure' in the CIA crash incident. (3) POLITICAL BATTLE LINES: The crisis has crystallized along Mexico's ruling vs. opposition axis — Morena backs Sheinbaum's federalized cooperation framework; PAN/PRI defend state-level bilateral security cooperation autonomy as constitutionally valid. The confrontation mirrors the underlying tension between federal sovereignty prerogatives and governors' historical discretion in bilateral security arrangements. SIGNIFICANCE FOR CJNG TRACKER: The April 28 Senate hearing is the highest-stakes institutional examination of U.S. intelligence operations on Mexican soil since the Tapalpa operation killed El Mencho. If the Senate establishes precedent that state-level CIA/DEA cooperation is categorically prohibited without federal authorization, the operational flexibility that produced the post-Tapalpa pressure campaign — including Operativo Enjambre (80+ arrests) — will face formal structural constraints in the 27 states with active CJNG presence. Sheinbaum's dual strategy remains: preserve the federal intelligence-sharing channel with Washington while constraining governor-level unilateral CIA/DEA cooperation.

media.caption: Infobae: Mexican Senate declares it essential to determine whether the federal government authorized CIA agents' participation in Chihuahua counter-narcotics raid
